title: Processor Clock Cycle Timeline
description: A visual timeline showing how a processor's clock signal drives the execution of instructions through a series of sequential stages.
relationships: The clock signal oscillates at a fixed frequency, defining the clock cycle. The rising edge of the clock typically triggers the start of a new processor operation (e.g., latching data into registers). Each stage of instruction execution (Fetch, Decode, Execute, etc.) is synchronized to complete within one or more clock cycles.
